# AI Builders Toolkit

[![Live site](https://img.shields.io/badge/live-agents.jrdevelopr.site-2563eb?logo=googlechrome&logoColor=white)](https://agents.jrdevelopr.site) [![Platforms](https://img.shields.io/badge/platforms-57-0e9f6e)](https://agents.jrdevelopr.site) [![Auto-updated weekly](https://img.shields.io/badge/auto--updated-weekly-7c3aed)](bin/discover.sh) [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue)](LICENSE)

> An **alphabetical, self-hosted-first** comparison of AI coding-agent platforms — find the best tool to **run and manage AI coding agents** from one place.

### ▶︎ Live, interactive version → **https://agents.jrdevelopr.site**

The goal: **one tool that does everything** — open an interactive shell, pull a GitHub issue, run any
agent CLI, and ship a PR, without tab-hopping to a separate terminal.

**Listed alphabetically** (A→Z) — no personal ranking. **Compared on:** **self-hosted** (runs on *your*
machines) · **subscription login** (sign in with your Claude Pro/Max & ChatGPT/Codex subscription instead of
metered API keys) · one-click **human shell** · **GitHub** issue sync + PRs/push · **many agents/models** (beyond
Claude & Codex) · **multiple logins** (separate client subscriptions) · **mobile** access · GitHub stars &
user sentiment. Free & self-hosted preferred; paid options are included and clearly marked.

**Legend:** ✅ native / strong · 🟡 partial / context-only · ➖ not documented &nbsp;|&nbsp;
*Self-hosted* ✅ = runs entirely on your own machines (not the vendor's cloud). *Sub login* ✅ = sign in with
your Claude Pro/Max & ChatGPT/Codex subscription (usually via the official Claude Code / Codex CLIs) instead of
per-token API keys; 🟡 = one side only or with caveats; ➖ = API key / BYOK only. *Multiple logins* = isolate
separate Claude/agent subscriptions per project. *Mobile* ✅ = app or mobile-first.

| # | Platform | ★ Stars | Cost | Sub login | Self-hosted | GitHub Issues | Projects | PRs / push | One-click shell | Multiple logins | Mobile | Agents / models | Bottom line (what users say) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **[1code](https://1code.dev)** | 5.6k | **Freemium** <br><sub>Apache-2.0 OSS; paid background agents</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Claude Code / Codex subscription logins, or your own API keys</sub> | ➖ | ➖ | 🟡 | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅<br><sub>PWA to monitor background agents</sub> | Claude Code, Codex; custom models/providers via own API keys | YC-backed; launched as a 'Cursor-like UI for Claude Code' on Show HN, with makers citing trust in parallel agents post-Opus 4.5. New project, so durable user sentiment is limited. |
| 2 | **[Agent Deck](https://github.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck)** | 323 | Free <br><sub>Free, MIT OSS</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Use your Claude Pro/Max & ChatGPT/Codex logins via the official Claude Code / Codex CLIs — no API key needed</sub> | ➖ | 🟡 | ➖ | ➖ | ✅ | ✅ | 🟡<br><sub>remote control via Telegram/Slack; local web UI</sub> | Claude Code, Gemini, OpenCode, Codex, Copilot, Crush, Cursor, Hermes, any terminal tool | A lightweight terminal session manager for juggling many agents in one TUI, with handy tmux integration and Telegram/Slack remote control. It's a session organizer rather than a full orchestrator, and is still small/early. |
| 3 | **[Agent Kanban](https://agent-kanban.dev)** | 362 | **Freemium** <br><sub>FSL-1.1 self-host free; hosted option</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Use your Claude Pro/Max & ChatGPT/Codex logins via the official Claude Code / Codex CLIs — no API key needed</sub> | ➖ | ➖ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ➖<br><sub>React web dashboard, no mobile app</sub> | Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Copilot CLI, any ACP agent | Appealing leader/worker autonomy model where agents claim cards and ship PRs end-to-end, and it appears on agent-orchestrator roundups. The FSL (non-Apache) license and newness are the main caveats. |
| 4 | **[AgentBox](https://agent-box.sh)** | 110 | Free <br><sub>MIT open-source</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Use your Claude Pro/Max & ChatGPT/Codex logins via the official Claude Code / Codex CLIs — no API key needed</sub> | ➖ | ➖ | ➖ | 🟡 | ✅ | ✅ | ➖<br><sub>not documented</sub> | Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode; model-agnostic | Strong on isolation — runs each agent in a sandboxed VM/container locally or in the cloud with FUSE overlays and persistent shells; the security-first design is its draw, though it's young and Docker-dependent. |

| 39 | **[OpenAgents Control](https://github.com/darrenhinde/OpenAgentsControl)** | 4.4k | Free <br><sub>MIT · open source</sub> | 🟡<br><sub>OpenCode providers — Anthropic OAuth works; most are API-key</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | 🟡 | ➖ | 🟡 | ➖ | ➖<br><sub>CLI / desktop only</sub> | Claude, GPT, Gemini, local — any OpenCode provider | Early but fast-growing OpenCode layer. Users like the approval gates and pattern/context system for consistent, ship-ready code on existing codebases; gripes: sequential approval is slower than autonomous tools and the per-project context files are a manual upfront setup. |
| 40 | **[OpenASE](https://github.com/PacificStudio/openase)** | 254 | Free <br><sub>Apache-2.0</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Use your Claude Pro/Max & ChatGPT/Codex logins via the official Claude Code / Codex CLIs — no API key needed</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | ✅ | 🟡 | 🟡 | ✅ | ➖<br><sub>Mobile / desktop apps on roadmap; web only now</sub> | Claude, Codex, Gemini CLI | New ticket-driven orchestration — Kanban ticket-to-code with multi-agent workflows and full traceability, self-hostable. Very new and unproven; small community. |
| 41 | **[OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw)** | 380k | Free <br><sub>open-source</sub> | 🟡<br><sub>Model-agnostic — subscription only where the underlying CLI supports it</sub> | ✅ | 🟡 | ➖ | 🟡 | 🟡 | 🟡 | 🟡<br><sub>Via chat channels (Slack/Telegram/…)</sub> | Model-agnostic; thousands of pre-built tools/workflows | Explosive 2026 breakout (~380k★) — self-hosted personal assistant plugging into WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/Discord via a huge connector ecosystem. Gripe: can rack up large API costs fast; chaotic rename history. |
| 42 | **[OpenCode](https://opencode.ai)** | 177k | Free <br><sub>open-source</sub> | 🟡<br><sub>Anthropic Pro/Max OAuth supported; most other providers are API-key</sub> | ✅ | ✅ | ➖ | ✅ | 🟡 | 🟡 | 🟡<br><sub>Mobile browser (web UI) / terminal</sub> | Any of 75+ providers incl. free & local models | Very popular open-source Claude Code alternative — model freedom (75+ providers incl. local) and a better terminal, often a cheaper daily driver. Gripe: recurring TUI/terminal bugs across platforms. |
| 43 | **[OpenKanban](https://github.com/techdufus/openkanban)** | 111 | Free <br><sub>AGPL-3.0 open-source</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Use your Claude Pro/Max & ChatGPT/Codex logins via the official Claude Code / Codex CLIs — no API key needed</sub> | ➖ | ➖ | 🟡 | ➖ | ✅ | ✅ | ➖<br><sub>terminal-only</sub> | OpenCode, Claude Code, Gemini, Codex, Aider, or any CLI | Neatly maps a kanban board onto agent orchestration, giving each ticket its own worktree and embedded terminal; a tidy mental model, but new, AGPL-licensed, and unproven at scale. |
| 44 | **[Orca](https://onorca.dev)** | 5.7k | Free <br><sub>MIT, OSS</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Use your Claude Pro/Max & ChatGPT/Codex logins via the official Claude Code / Codex CLIs — no API key needed</sub> | ➖ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅<br><sub>iOS + Android companion apps</sub> | Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, OpenCode, Amp, Grok, Goose, Cline, Droid, Kilocode + any CLI agent | Early positive buzz as a powerful parallel-agent ADE; one user said they 'cannot believe how good Orca is.' Detailed long-term reviews are still scarce given its newness. |
| 45 | **[Paperclip](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip)** | 71k | Free <br><sub>MIT</sub> | 🟡<br><sub>Via adapters — the Claude Code adapter uses its login; others are API</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | ✅ | ➖ | ➖ | ✅ | 🟡<br><sub>Web (responsive) control plane</sub> | Any agent/provider/runtime via adapters | Viral "AI company / org-chart" orchestrator — slick all-in-one dashboard of agents, goals, budgets and cost tracking. Gripe: polarizing — critics call the zero-human-company premise a gimmick with no quality control. |
| 46 | **[parallel-code](https://parallelcode.app)** | 740 | Free <br><sub>Free, MIT OSS</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Use your Claude Pro/Max & ChatGPT/Codex logins via the official Claude Code / Codex CLIs — no API key needed</sub> | ➖ | ➖ | 🟡 | 🟡 | ✅ | ✅ | ✅<br><sub>monitor from phone via QR over Wi-Fi/Tailscale</sub> | Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, Antigravity, Copilot CLI | Clean keyboard-first worktree manager from the Super Productivity author; the phone-monitoring and per-task terminals get praise. Still young and Mac/Linux-only (no Windows), with limited public sentiment so far. |
| 47 | **[Paseo](https://github.com/getpaseo/paseo)** | 8.9k | Free <br><sub>open-source</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Drives the agent CLIs' own logins (Claude Pro/Max, ChatGPT/Codex)</sub> | ✅ | 🟡 | ➖ | 🟡 | ✅ | 🟡 | ✅<br><sub>Mobile client (iOS/Android) + desktop/web/CLI</sub> | Dozens of coding-agent CLIs | Strong HN reception as a privacy-first multi-device orchestrator — phone control called "life-changing", no telemetry, multi-provider, voice mode. Gripe: "beautiful" marketing disputed; users want cost tracking to curb token waste. |
| 48 | **[RCFlow](https://www.rcflow.app)** | 1 | Free <br><sub>AGPL</sub> | 🟡<br><sub>Runs the CLIs (sub-capable) but is documented with an Anthropic API key</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | ✅ | ➖ | ✅ | 🟡 | ✅<br><sub>Mobile client (+ desktop)</sub> | Claude, Codex, OpenCode (Anthropic/Bedrock/OpenAI-compat) | Very new coding-agent backend pitching split-view multi-agent sessions, low-lag WebSockets and session persistence. Unproven — no community footprint yet. |
| 49 | **[Sandcastle](https://github.com/mattpocock/sandcastle)** | 6.3k | Free <br><sub>MIT, open-source</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Use your Claude Pro/Max & ChatGPT/Codex logins via the official Claude Code / Codex CLIs — no API key needed</sub> | ➖ | 🟡 | ➖ | 🟡 | ✅ | ✅ | ➖<br><sub></sub> | Claude Code, Codex, Pi, Cursor, OpenCode, Copilot | Well-regarded as the most principled local isolation solution for unattended agents (high audit scores); gripe is it's pre-1.0 with a still-moving API, so pin versions carefully. |
| 50 | **[Sortie](https://github.com/sortie-ai/sortie)** | 82 | Free <br><sub>Apache-2.0</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Use your Claude Pro/Max & ChatGPT/Codex logins via the official Claude Code / Codex CLIs — no API key needed</sub> | ✅ | ✅ | 🟡 | 🟡 | ➖ | ➖ | ➖<br><sub>Headless daemon — no UI</sub> | Claude, Codex, Copilot, OpenCode, Kiro | Niche headless issue-to-agent orchestrator — single Go binary, tracker-agnostic, isolated workspaces with retries and cost tracking; honest that it only orchestrates. Tiny adoption, little discussion. |
| 51 | **[Supacode](https://supacode.sh)** | 1.5k | Free <br><sub>Free (beta), open source</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Use your Claude Pro/Max & ChatGPT/Codex logins via the official Claude Code / Codex CLIs — no API key needed</sub> | ➖ | ➖ | 🟡 | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ➖<br><sub>macOS only</sub> | Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode — any CLI agent | A fast native macOS command center built on libghostty, liked for its no-Electron snappiness and worktree isolation; downsides are it is macOS-26-only and still labeled beta. |
| 52 | **[Superset](https://superset.sh)** | 12k | Free <br><sub>Elastic License 2.0</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Use your Claude Pro/Max & ChatGPT/Codex logins via the official Claude Code / Codex CLIs — no API key needed</sub> | ➖ | ➖ | ➖ | 🟡 | ✅ | 🟡 | ➖<br><sub>desktop-only</sub> | Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, Copilot, Amp, Droid, OpenCode, Pi + any CLI agent | Built by ex-YC CTOs; fans on HN/PH call running 10+ parallel agents a 'paradigm shift' and praise the local-only, no-telemetry design; skeptics counter that it just converts typing time into (worse) reading time, and Windows/Linux builds are still untested. |
| 53 | **[SwarmClaw](https://swarmclaw.ai)** | 587 | Free <br><sub>MIT</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Runs the CLIs (sub) — or any of 24 API providers</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | ➖ | 🟡 | 🟡 | 🟡 | 🟡<br><sub>Mobile-friendly responsive web</sub> | Claude, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini, Copilot, Cursor, Qwen, Goose + 24 providers | Early OSS multi-agent runtime — broad provider support (23+ LLMs), MCP tools, memory and scheduling, easy npm install. Obscure and unproven; almost no real-world reviews. |
| 54 | **[Symphony](https://openai.com/index/open-source-codex-orchestration-symphony/)** | 26k | Free <br><sub>Apache-2.0</sub> | 🟡<br><sub>ChatGPT/Codex subscription via the Codex CLI; no Claude</sub> | ➖ | 🟡 | ✅ | ✅ | ➖ | ➖ | ➖<br><sub>none</sub> | OpenAI Codex (per-issue agents) | HN interest is cautious — praised as a clean blueprint for issue-driven agent orchestration (claims of ~5x landed PRs), but OpenAI labels it an unmaintained 'engineering preview' that's really just a SPEC.md, so it needs real harness work to use. |
| 55 | **[t3code](https://t3.codes)** | 13k | Free <br><sub>MIT (BYOK)</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Use your Claude Pro/Max & ChatGPT/Codex logins via the official Claude Code / Codex CLIs — no API key needed</sub> | ➖ | ➖ | ➖ | ✅ | ➖ | 🟡 | 🟡<br><sub>web GUI (also self-host web server)</sub> | Codex, Claude, Cursor, OpenCode (more coming) | Theo's free GUI got 12k+ stars fast and is praised as the first agent GUI that isn't sluggish, with a clean commit-to-PR flow; gripes are real bugs (tilde paths, missing diff views, no web-server auth) and it can run slower than Codex directly — README literally says 'expect bugs.' |
| 56 | **[Vibe Kanban](https://github.com/BloopAI/vibe-kanban)** | 27k | Free <br><sub>open-source · sunsetting</sub> | ✅<br><sub>Use your Claude Pro/Max & ChatGPT/Codex logins via the official Claude Code / Codex CLIs — no API key needed</sub> | ✅ | 🟡 | ✅ | 🟡 | ✅ | 🟡 | 🟡<br><sub>Web (responsive)</sub> | Claude, Codex, Gemini, Copilot, Cursor, OpenCode, Qwen + | Well-liked kanban over Claude Code/Codex/Gemini with isolated worktrees for parallel work. Major caveat: BloopAI is sunsetting it — future rests on community maintenance. |

Single-agent (Claude-only) and a smaller project, so it's narrower than the multi-agent orchestrators here. |

---

### How this stays current
The table is generated from the data in **[`site/index.html`](site/index.html)** (a single `PLATFORMS`
array). A weekly bot scans curated lists of new agent platforms, adds any that are missing, re-ranks,
and regenerates both the live site and this README — so they never drift.

**Suggest a platform:** [open an issue](https://github.com/jrdevelopr/ai-builders-toolkit/issues/new?title=Suggest%20a%20platform:%20).

<sub>Ratings are based on official docs/repos; “➖”/“🟡” are used instead of guessing. Projects = the platform's own
project/task board or PM-tool integration (Linear, Jira, GitHub Projects). Prices are list-price estimates and change often — verify before buying.
This is a personal, independent comparison, free to share.</sub>
